title: The Book of Quinte Essence
summary: The checked-in XML is the pinned General Corpus copy of University of Michigan CME item ANZ4356. Its source description identifies Frederick J. Furnivall's revised 1889 edition, and its inclusion of the 1889 Contents and separately added Spheres and Planets confirms that revised textual state, although the encoded Oxford University Press title page leaves the exact later carrier or reprint unresolved. Furnivall's target text on pages 1-25 directly transcribes British Library Sloane MS 73, ff. 10r-25v, with proof read against that manuscript by Edmund Brock; Harley MS 853, ff. 66r-73v, was consulted only for a few passages. The Sloane and Harley texts represent a condensed Middle English translation-adaptation of John of Rupescissa's 1351-1352 Latin Liber de consideratione quintae essentiae. The 1866 first edition is an earlier state, not the U-M encoding's immediate source, and Halversen's 1998 dissertation is supporting scholarship rather than an ancestor. Complete scans of both Furnivall states and supporting scholarship are cached. No authorized public complete facsimile of Sloane MS 73 or Harley MS 853 was verified on 2026-07-15; current British Library catalog, onsite consultation, paid photography, and permission routes are recorded instead.
review_notes: Hermes Trismegistus is an inherited legendary attribution in the Middle English incipit, not the accepted author of either the Latin source or this anonymous English translation-adaptation.
The Spheres and Planets is a separate medieval text from Sloane MS 73, f. 26, appended to the edition in 1889; it is not an intrinsic third book of The Book of Quinte Essence.

The revised edition's glossary on pages 29-31 is listed in the encoded contents but omitted from the XML. The encoding relation therefore covers selected portions of the revised volume, not the full printed carrier.

Halversen gives Sloane MS 73, ff. 11-25v, while Furnivall's folio milestones and the current British Library record begin the target at f. 10. The current record and edition milestones control the principal locator, with the discrepancy preserved as an open question.
Internet Archive labels its first-edition scan 1889, but direct inspection of the title page establishes 1866 and the volume lacks the additions that Furnivall expressly says were made in 1889.

entities.label: General Corpus source XML for anz4356
entities.description: The exact XML artifact consumed by this repository's conversion workflow and supplied through the pinned Kyvero-Press/CME git submodule.
notes: The pinned file is 71,288 bytes.

entities.label: University of Michigan Corpus of Middle English Prose and Verse item ANZ4356
entities.description: A 2006 Level 4 TEI-in-Libraries encoding of a later Oxford University Press carrier preserving Furnivall's revised 1889 textual state. It includes the edition title page; introduction; 1889 contents; target Books I-II on printed pages 1-25; the separately appended Spheres and Planets on page 26; and Gill's notes on pages 27-28. It omits the revised edition's glossary on pages 29-31.
notes: The PROFILEDESC declares eng, but the target medieval work is Middle English with brief embedded Latin; modern English belongs to edition paratext.
The XML SOURCEDESC specifies the N. Trübner revised 1889 edition, whereas the encoded title page gives a later Oxford University Press London-New York-Toronto imprint without a reprint date.

entities.label: The Book of Quinte Essence (abstract Middle English work)
alternate_labels: The Fifth Being; That Is to Say, Man's Heaven
entities.description: The anonymous fifteenth-century Middle English condensed translation and adaptation of John of Rupescissa's Latin quintessence treatise, distinguished here from manuscript witnesses, scholarly editions, facsimiles, encodings, and the separately appended Spheres and Planets.
notes: The incipit's Hermes attribution is legendary. No translator is named and no physical Latin exemplar for this version is identified.

entities.label: John of Rupescissa, Liber de consideratione quintae essentiae omnium rerum
entities.description: The two-book Latin work on quintessence composed by John of Rupescissa in 1351-1352: Book I develops the alchemical theory and manufacture of quintessence, and Book II presents medical applications. It is the intellectual source work behind the Middle English translation traditions, not an identified physical exemplar in this chain.

entities.label: The Spheres and Planets
entities.description: A short anonymous Middle English astrological account of the celestial spheres and planets at Sloane MS 73, f. 26. Furnivall appended it to the revised edition on page 26 in 1889; it is modeled as a separate related work, not part of the target's intrinsic two-book structure.

entities.label: Frederick J. Furnivall's revised edition of The Book of Quinte Essence (1889)
entities.description: The revised state underlying the U-M encoding. Its title page retains the 1866 imprint with a bracketed Revised, 1889 statement; Furnivall's postscript says the Contents on pages vii-viii and The Spheres on page 26 were then added. The target occupies pages 1-25, Gill's notes pages 27-28, and the glossary pages 29-31.

bibliographic.citation: Frederick J. Furnivall, ed., The Book of Quinte Essence; or, The Fifth Being; That Is to Say, Man's Heaven, Early English Text Society, Original Series 16 (London: N. Trübner & Co., 1866 [revised 1889]), viii + 31 pp.
bibliographic.title: The Book of Quinte Essence; or, The Fifth Being; That Is to Say, Man's Heaven
bibliographic.series: Early English Text Society, Original Series 16
bibliographic.publisher: N. Trübner & Co. for the Early English Text Society

notes: Furnivall identifies Sloane MS 73 as base text, credits Edmund Brock with reading proof against it, and says Harley MS 853 was collated only for a few passages requiring elucidation.
The complete revised scan was recovered from Wikimedia Commons after its Internet Archive/Digital Library of India source item became access-restricted.

entities.label: Frederick J. Furnivall's first edition of The Book of Quinte Essence (1866)
entities.description: The first printed edition and prior state of Furnivall's text. Direct title-page inspection establishes 1866. It contains the target on pages 1-25 but lacks the 1889 Contents and Spheres additions; it proceeds from the target's ending to Gill's notes and the glossary.

bibliographic.citation: Frederick J. Furnivall, ed., The Book of Quinte Essence; or, The Fifth Being (London: N. Trübner & Co. for the Early English Text Society, 1866), Early English Text Society, Original Series 16.
bibliographic.title: The Book of Quinte Essence; or, The Fifth Being
bibliographic.series: Early English Text Society, Original Series 16
bibliographic.publisher: N. Trübner & Co. for the Early English Text Society

notes: Internet Archive metadata gives 1889 despite the inspected 1866 title page; that provider date is preserved as contradicted provider metadata, not adopted as the edition date.

entities.label: Wikimedia Commons complete scan of Furnivall's revised 1889 edition
entities.description: A complete 46-page PDF page-image scan of the revised volume, derived from the Digital Library of India item dli.granth.77364 and made publicly downloadable through Wikimedia Commons.

entities.label: Internet Archive complete scan of Furnivall's 1866 first edition
entities.description: A complete 46-page PDF scan digitized in 2011 with University of Toronto funding, with OCR, OCR XML, page-number mapping, scandata, and provider metadata derivatives.
entities.reproduction_format: Complete PDF page-image scan with OCR and page-structure derivatives

entities.label: British Library, Sloane MS 73, ff. 10r-25v
entities.description: The fifteenth-century English base witness directly transcribed by Furnivall. The target is the item cataloged as Hermes Trismegistus, De quintis essentiis, at ff. 10-25; Furnivall's edition milestones run from 10a through 25b, interpreted as ff. 10r-25v. The separately cataloged astrology text The Spheres and Planets follows at f. 26.

date_statements.label: Target item date in current catalog
date_statements.notes: The current record gives only a century for the target item. Furnivall reports E. A. Bond's narrower circa 1460-1470 estimate.

date_statements.label: Furnivall and E. A. Bond estimate

holding.notes: The target occupies ff. 10r-25v under the current record's ff. 10-25 description; Spheres and Planets is a separate item at f. 26.

physical_description.extent: Current interim record reports one item but does not supply a complete folio count
notes: The current record assigns the whole composite a broad 10th-18th-century range; that range is not a date for the target folios.
The record lists Dutch, English, French, and Latin for the composite.
No public complete manuscript facsimile was verified on 2026-07-15.
Halversen's ff. 11-25v locator conflicts with the current catalog and Furnivall's printed milestones; no silent harmonization is made.

entities.label: British Library Archives and Manuscripts record 040-002112411
entities.description: The current interim whole-manuscript catalog record for Sloane MS 73, including selected contents, identifiers, languages, broad composite dating, physical-request information, and an empty Digitised Content field.

entities.label: British Library, Harley MS 853, ff. 66r-73v
entities.description: A collateral later witness to the condensed Middle English version. The current catalog identifies the liber quintae essentiae at ff. 66r-73v and records its ending date as 22 August 1589. Furnivall says this copy was collated only for a few passages requiring elucidation, so it is not the base witness or a direct ancestor of the whole edition.

date_statements.label: Current whole-manuscript date
date_statements.notes: The target's explicit 1589 date is more specific than the catalog's approximate date for the composite volume.

physical_description.extent: f. 1* + 152 folios, with four unfoliated paper flyleaves at the beginning and five at the end; target ff. 66r-73v
notes: Written in Gothic cursive and produced in England.
The post-1600 British Museum binding was renewed on 28 September 1966.
No public complete manuscript facsimile was verified on 2026-07-15.
The current dated ending supersedes Furnivall and Bond's rough about-1600 estimate.

entities.label: British Library Archives and Manuscripts record 040-002046682
entities.description: The current official whole-manuscript record for Harley MS 853, including a detailed contents list, target incipit and date, physical description, provenance, physical-request information, and an empty Digitised Content field.

entities.label: Marguerite A. Halversen, The Consideration of Quintessence (1998 dissertation)
entities.description: A modern critical edition and study of a different Middle English textual state, based principally on Ferguson MS 205 and Sloane MS 353. Its witness census and prior-edition discussion analyze Sloane MS 73 and Harley MS 853 as the condensed second version. It supports the lineage claims but is not an ancestor of Furnivall's edition or the U-M encoding.

bibliographic.citation: Marguerite A. Halversen, The Consideration of Quintessence: An Edition of a Middle English Translation of John of Rupescissa's Liber de Consideratione de Quintae Essentiae Omnium Rerum with Introduction, Notes and Commentary (PhD diss., Michigan State University, 1998), xii + 484 pp., doi:10.25335/bav5-r656.
bibliographic.title: The Consideration of Quintessence

access.contact: British Library Digitisation Services; permissions@bl.uk or licensing@bl.uk for written reuse permission
access.format: Manuscript photography; original manuscripts cannot be scanned
notes: For a complete-object facsimile, request covers or boards, spine, pastedowns, every unfoliated flyleaf and blank, both sides of every folio in the current object, edges where available, final pastedown, and any detached matter. Ask staff to confirm the full current foliation and image inventory because the interim record does not provide it.

Associate the target separately with ff. 10r-25v and The Spheres and Planets with f. 26r-v. A request limited to those leaves would be a partial work-focused reproduction, not a complete manuscript facsimile.
Delivery does not itself grant republication rights; request written permission and exact repository/publication terms separately.

rights.component: Underlying fifteenth-century Middle English verbal work
rights.jurisdiction: United States and jurisdictions where historical copyright terms have expired
rights.basis: The anonymous verbal work is attested in fifteenth-century manuscripts and was first published in 1866; this component excludes modern editions, encodings, metadata, and photography.
notes: This is a component- and jurisdiction-specific research assessment, not a worldwide guarantee about every reproduction or contribution.

rights.jurisdiction: United States and jurisdictions where historical copyright terms have expired
rights.basis: John of Rupescissa composed the source work in 1351-1352; this component excludes modern editions, translations, and reproductions.

rights.component: Repository-contributed wrapping, integration metadata, and original additions, if any, excluding copied U-M text and markup
rights.jurisdiction: Worldwide dedication to the extent legally possible
rights.basis: The pinned CME repository LICENSE is the Unlicense and can dedicate contributor-owned original repository contributions to the public domain to the extent possible. The evidence does not establish that CME contributors own or can relicense the copied U-M transcription, encoding, or markup inside this XML.
notes: The repository license does not automatically transfer to upstream U-M text or markup, underlying manuscript images, third-party edition scans, provider metadata, or modern scholarship.

The byte-level boundary between any original CME contribution and copied U-M content has not been established; the U-M encoding's separate copyright-may-apply statement therefore remains controlling for that upstream layer.

rights.component: University of Michigan electronic encoding and editorial markup
rights.basis: The XML header states that where applicable the item is subject to copyright and that other distribution restrictions may apply; it supplies no item-specific open license.
notes: This provider statement is kept separate from the Unlicense covering the pinned CME repository artifact.

editorial_practices.source_feature: Readings in the later Harley MS 853 copy

editorial_practices.description: Furnivall's 1866 preface identifies Sloane MS 73 as base and says Harley MS 853 was collated only for a few passages requiring elucidation. The revised 1889 state inherits this limited collation.
editorial_practices.scope: Selected passages in the target on edition pp. 1-25; exact complete list not supplied

editorial_practices.source_feature: Material absent from the 1866 first state
editorial_practices.representation: Contents on pp. vii-viii and the separate Sloane f. 26 Spheres text on p. 26 were added in 1889
editorial_practices.description: Furnivall's dated postscript explicitly identifies the two additions, and direct comparison of the complete first and revised scans confirms them.
editorial_practices.scope: Revised edition front matter and separately appended p. 26

editorial_practices.representation: The XML contents list retains the Glossary entry, but the encoded body ends after Gill's notes on p. 28
editorial_practices.description: The U-M encoding represents the revised volume only through page 28 and omits its glossary. Whether this was deliberate selection or accidental incompleteness is unresolved.

evidence.citation: Pinned General Corpus XML for University of Michigan CME item ANZ4356.
evidence.summary: Supports the exact repository file and hashes, UMDL identifier, 2006 encoding statement, source description naming Revised, 1889, the later OUP carrier title page, encoded page and folio milestones, two intrinsic books, separate Spheres section, Gill notes, rights warning, and contents-listed but omitted glossary.

evidence.citation: Structural inspection of the pinned ANZ4356 XML.
evidence.summary: Counts one TITLEPAGE, four DIV1 elements, three DIV2 elements, 68 P elements within TEXT, 20 NOTE1 elements, 33 PB elements, and 34 manuscript-folio MILESTONE elements; identifies ANZ4356:3.1 and 3.2 as Books I-II, ANZ4356:3.3 as Spheres, and ANZ4356:4 as Gill's notes.

evidence.citation: University of Michigan Library, Corpus of Middle English Prose and Verse, item ANZ4356.
evidence.summary: Provides the public canonical U-M item route and confirms the ANZ4356 discovery identifier. The exact encoding is independently auditable through the pinned repository XML.
notes: Automated retrieval encountered the current anti-bot interstitial; the official item remains indexed and the repository XML supplies its content.

evidence.citation: Frederick J. Furnivall, ed., The Book of Quinte Essence, revised 1889 state, complete Wikimedia Commons scan.
evidence.summary: Establishes the complete revised state, including the bracketed Revised, 1889 title-page statement, Furnivall's introduction and 1889 postscript, newly added contents, target pp. 1-25, separate Spheres p. 26, Gill notes pp. 27-28, and glossary pp. 29-31.

evidence.citation: Direct page-by-page inspection of the cached complete revised 1889 Furnivall scan.
evidence.locator: PDF pages covering title leaf, pp. v-viii, and printed pp. 1-31
evidence.summary: Confirms that the scan is complete, that the target ends on printed p. 25, that Spheres begins separately on p. 26, and that the printed glossary omitted from XML is physically present on pp. 29-31.

evidence.citation: Wikimedia Commons API metadata for the revised Furnivall scan file.
evidence.summary: Confirms a 46-page, 1,840,828-byte PDF; credits the Digital Library of India/Asiatic Society of Mumbai/Government of Maharashtra provenance; and reports Public domain, Copyrighted False, and no attribution requirement.

evidence.citation: Frederick J. Furnivall, ed., The Book of Quinte Essence (London: N. Trübner & Co., 1866), complete Internet Archive scan.
evidence.locator: title page; preface pp. v-vi; target pp. 1-25; back matter
evidence.summary: The title page reads MDCCCLXVI. The preface identifies Sloane MS 73 as base, credits Brock's proof-reading, and limits Harley collation to a few passages. The volume has the target on pp. 1-25 but lacks the contents and Spheres additions made in 1889.

evidence.citation: Direct page-by-page inspection of the cached complete Furnivall first-edition scan.
evidence.locator: title page, preface, target ending, and following back matter
evidence.summary: Confirms the physical 1866 title page and the absence of both an editorial contents section and The Spheres and Planets, contradicting the provider's 1889 date while establishing the first-edition boundary.

evidence.citation: Internet Archive metadata for item bookofquinteesse00furn.
evidence.summary: Supplies item identifiers, derivative inventory, scan funding and provenance, and a provider date of 1889. The date is preserved as provider metadata but contradicted by direct inspection of the scanned 1866 title page and contents.

evidence.citation: British Library, Archives and Manuscripts, Sloane MS 73, record 040-002112411.
evidence.locator: ff. 10-25; f. 26; holding and access fields; Digitised Content
evidence.summary: Confirms Sloane MS 73 identifiers and holding; the fifteenth-century English De quintis essentiis at ff. 10-25; the separate fifteenth-century Of the spheres and planets at f. 26; the composite's broad date/language context; physical-request route; blank Digitised Content field; and CC BY licensing for catalog text.
notes: This is the current interim catalog after the British Library cyberattack and may omit legacy detail.

evidence.citation: British Library, Archives and Manuscripts, Harley MS 853, record 040-002046682.
evidence.locator: ff. 66r-73v; physical description; origin; holding and access fields; Digitised Content
evidence.summary: Confirms the liber quintae essentiae at ff. 66r-73v, incipit, ending date 22 August 1589, whole-manuscript circa-1575 catalog date, English origin, paper support, dimensions, foliation, physical-request route, blank Digitised Content field, and CC BY licensing for catalog text.

evidence.citation: Dated search for complete authorized public facsimiles of British Library Sloane MS 73 and Harley MS 853.
evidence.summary: Exact records, exact-shelfmark searches, current digitised-manuscripts guidance, and broader public repository searches exposed no complete authorized public facsimile for either manuscript on 2026-07-15. Both exact record Digitised Content fields are empty.
notes: A negative public-access finding does not establish that no unpublished, selected, legacy, internal, or private photographs exist.

evidence.citation: Marguerite A. Halversen, The Consideration of Quintessence: An Edition of a Middle English Translation of John of Rupescissa's Liber de Consideratione de Quintae Essentiae Omnium Rerum (PhD diss., Michigan State University, 1998).
evidence.locator: PDF pp. 87-92; dissertation pp. 72-77; copyright page
evidence.summary: Supports John of Rupescissa's authorship and 1351-1352 Latin composition; the two-book structure; the English witness census and version families; Sloane MS 73 and Harley MS 853 as the condensed second version; its omissions and rearrangements; Furnivall's edition history; and Halversen's 1998 copyright notice.
notes: Halversen gives Sloane MS 73, ff. 11-25v, in conflict with current BL and Furnivall locators. Her own critical edition uses Ferguson MS 205 and Sloane MS 353 and is not a source ancestor of the repository text.

evidence.citation: Michigan State University Digital Repository MODS record for ETD 47644.
evidence.locator: titleInfo; name; originInfo; physicalDescription; subjects; accessCondition; recordInfo
evidence.summary: Confirms title, author, Michigan State University PhD status, 1998 date, xii + 484-page extent, subject headings, OCLC 1083367512, and In Copyright rights statement.

evidence.citation: Middle English Dictionary source stencil, a1475 Bk.Quint.(Sln 73).
evidence.summary: Supports the MED's a1475 scholarly dating and source abbreviation for Furnivall's Sloane MS 73 text; this is retained separately from the current BL century date and Furnivall/Bond's circa 1460-1470 estimate.

evidence.citation: Kyvero-Press/CME repository LICENSE at pinned commit e1d77c88ca561be924b2fedc54dd474be2e7e49c (The Unlicense).
evidence.summary: Supports the Unlicense public-domain dedication for the repository artifact and repository contributions; it does not purport to relicense third-party manuscript images, scans, or scholarship.

Supplies the current paid online order route and explains quotation, preservation assessment, item identifiers, delivery choices, and that original manuscripts cannot be scanned and may instead require photography.

evidence.citation: British Library, Imaging Services terms, updated January 2026.
evidence.summary: Supports preservation-suitability review, fees, delivery restrictions, third-party-rights caution, single-page imaging conditions, and the requirement for written permission before copying, publishing, or reproducing supplied material.

evidence.citation: British Library, Digitisation Services rate card 2025/26.
evidence.summary: Supplies current service categories and indicative GBP charges. A complete manuscript request remains quotation-based and may be substantially more expensive than a small folio selection.

open_questions.question: Which exact later Oxford University Press reprint or carrier copy did U-M encode?
next_steps: Ask U-M for the source-copy catalog record or page images.
Compare the XML title-page imprint and typography against dated EETS reprints.
notes: The encoded content securely reflects the revised 1889 state, but textual state and physical carrier are not the same claim.

open_questions.question: When and where was the condensed Middle English version made, and which Latin exemplar or intermediary did its anonymous translator-adapter use?
next_steps: Review Halversen's complete stemmatic and textual analysis and later scholarship.
Seek dialectal localization and any proposed Latin exemplar.

open_questions.question: Why does Halversen describe the Sloane target as ff. 11-25v while Furnivall's folio milestones and the current British Library record begin it at f. 10?
next_steps: Inspect a complete Sloane MS 73 facsimile or obtain British Library confirmation.
Check historical and current foliation for renumbering or offset.

open_questions.question: Can complete authorized digital facsimiles of Sloane MS 73 and Harley MS 853 be obtained, and what exact repository or publication reuse terms would govern them?
next_steps: Submit separate British Library complete-object digitisation enquiries and request item-specific quotations.
Ask for a confirmed complete image inventory and written Git repository, research, publication, and commercial-reuse terms before redistribution.

open_questions.question: Exactly which Harley MS 853 readings were collated into Furnivall's first edition and inherited by the revised state?
next_steps: Audit every Furnivall variant note marked MS. Harl. against the complete first and revised editions.
Compare those passages with an authorized Harley MS 853 facsimile if obtained.

open_questions.question: Was the revised edition's glossary deliberately excluded from the U-M encoding, or is the current XML incomplete?
next_steps: Compare any alternate U-M delivery files or archived documentation.
Ask U-M whether the page-range selection was intentional.

open_questions.question: Which portions, if any, of the pinned CME XML are original repository contributions covered by the Unlicense rather than copied U-M text or markup whose reuse authority remains unresolved?
next_steps: Trace the import and modification history for anz4356.xml in the CME repository.
Seek clarification from CME maintainers and U-M about ownership and reuse authority for the transcription and encoding layers.
notes: The Unlicense is recorded only for contributor-owned repository additions; it is not treated as a dedication of copied third-party content.
